title: ds-interactivity - core option matrix (light)
description: Combined test bench for the 5 interactivity skills that share an absolute layout: ds-tokens, ds-inputs, ds-defaults, ds-drilldowns, ds-visibility. Each section tests one skill. Tabs have their own dashboard because tabbed layout cannot coexist with absolute layout. SPL is makeresults-based so this runs anywhere.

options.query: | makeresults count=5 | streamstats count AS row | eval host=case(row=1,"web-01",row=2,"web-02",row=3,"db-01",row=4,"app-01",row=5,"cache-01"), action=case(row%2=0,"allow",1=1,"deny"), bytes=row*1024 | table host action bytes
| makeresults | eval host="$selected_host$", note="clicked from upstream table" | table host note

options.markdown:
# `ds-interactivity` core matrix

One dashboard, five interactivity skills. Each section is owned by one SKILL.md.

- §1 **ds-tokens** — token output panel
- §2 **ds-inputs** — input flavours (timerange, dropdown, multiselect, text, dynamic dropdown)
- §3 **ds-defaults** — global time wiring
- §4 **ds-drilldowns** — setToken / linkToDashboard / linkToSearch / customUrl
- §5 **ds-visibility** — show / hide based on token state

## §1 ds-tokens

The panel below shows every token resolved end-to-end. Change any input above and watch the string update.

viz_token_echo.title: Token echo
viz_token_echo.description: Markdown panel that interpolates every dashboard token directly. No SPL involved — the markdown engine substitutes `$tok$` references at render time. Token values are wrapped in inline-code spans so wildcards and special chars (e.g. `*`, `_`) don't get reinterpreted as markdown formatting.

options.markdown:
**Resolved SPL fragment:**

```spl
| eval msg="index=$selected_index$ status IN ($status|s$) filter=$search_text$ host=$host_filter$ time=$global_time.earliest$..$global_time.latest$"
```

**Individual tokens:**

- `$selected_index$` → `$selected_index$`
- `$status$` → `$status$`    (multiselect; with `|s` → `$status|s$`)
- `$search_text$` → `$search_text$`
- `$host_filter$` → `$host_filter$`
- `$global_time.earliest$` → `$global_time.earliest$`
- `$global_time.latest$` → `$global_time.latest$`
- `$selected_host$` (from §4 click) → `$selected_host$`
- `$selected_action$` (from §4 click) → `$selected_action$`

## §2 ds-inputs

5 input flavours wired above:
- `input.timerange` — `global_time` (with `.earliest` / `.latest` accessors)
- `input.dropdown` — `selected_index`
- `input.multiselect` — `status` (array, comma-joined in token expansion)
- `input.text` — `search_text`
- `input.dropdown` w/ DOS items — `host_filter` (populated from `ds_hosts`)

viz_inputs_proof.title: Inputs → tokens (live)
viz_inputs_proof.description: Markdown table that maps each input above to the token it writes and the current token value. Live-updates as the user changes any input.

options.markdown:
| Input | Type | Token | Current value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Time range | `input.timerange` | `global_time` | earliest=`$global_time.earliest$`   latest=`$global_time.latest$` |
| Index | `input.dropdown` | `selected_index` | `$selected_index$` |
| Status | `input.multiselect` | `status` | `$status$`   (with `|s` filter: `$status|s$`) |
| Search filter | `input.text` | `search_text` | `$search_text$` |
| Host (dynamic) | `input.dropdown` w/ DOS items | `host_filter` | `$host_filter$` |

**From drilldowns (§4):**

| Source | Token | Current value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Row click → `setToken` | `selected_host` | `$selected_host$` |
| Row click → `setToken` | `selected_action` | `$selected_action$` |

## §3 ds-defaults

The `defaults.dataSources."ds.search".options.queryParameters` block wires `$global_time.earliest$` / `$global_time.latest$` into every search above. None of the `ds.search` entries set their own `queryParameters` — the default flows through.

viz_kpi_default_time.title: KPI driven by defaults
viz_kpi_default_time.description: This `ds.search` declares no queryParameters. The dashboard-level defaults block wires global_time into it.

options.markdown:
## §4 ds-drilldowns

Click a row in the table below to fire the demo drilldown.

- **setToken** — captures `row.host.value` into `$selected_host$` and `row.action.value` into `$selected_action$`. §5 reacts to `$selected_host$`.
- **linkToSearch** — opens the Splunk Search UI prefilled with `index=main host=<row>` and forwards the dashboard's current time range.
- **linkToDashboard** — re-navigates to this same dashboard (`ds_interactivity_core_dark`), forwarding `selected_host` + the time range. **Token forwarding requires the array-of-objects shape** `tokens: [{token, value}]` — the string-keyed map shape is silently rejected by the editor.
- **customUrl** — opens an external investigation URL with URL-encoded row values via the `|u` token filter.

viz_drilldown_table.title: Drilldown source
viz_drilldown_table.description: Click any row to fire all four drilldown actions. setToken populates $selected_host$ for §5.

viz_drilldown_table_url.title: Drilldown — customUrl
viz_drilldown_table_url.description: Click a row to open an external URL with URL-encoded row values via |u filter.

viz_drilldown_table_search.title: Drilldown — linkToSearch
viz_drilldown_table_search.description: Opens Splunk Search with a prefilled query and the dashboard's current time range.

options.query: index=main host=$row.host.value$ | stats count by sourcetype

viz_drilldown_table_dash.title: Drilldown — linkToDashboard
viz_drilldown_table_dash.description: Navigates to ds_interactivity_core_dark in the same app, forwarding row.host.value as the `selected_host` token and the current global time range. Uses the array-of-objects `tokens: [{token, value}]` shape (the documented schema). The string-keyed `tokens: { name: value }` shape is rejected by the editor with no error.

options.markdown:
## §5 ds-visibility

Three panels below toggle on different token states of `$selected_host$`. Click rows in §4 to flip them on and off.

- **panel A** — visible when `$selected_host$` is set (after any row click in §4). Condition: `$selected_host$ != ""`.
- **panel B** — visible only when `$selected_host$` equals `web-01`. Condition: `$selected_host$ = "web-01"`.
- **panel C** — visible when `$selected_host$` is unset (initial state). Condition: `$selected_host$ = ""`.

**How the wiring works:** every panel's `containerOptions.visibility.showConditions` references one or more *condition IDs*. The conditions themselves are declared at the dashboard root under `expressions.conditions.<id>.value`.

**Two hard rules from QA debugging:**

1. **`visibility` lives under `containerOptions`, NOT at panel root.** Setting `visibility` directly on the viz is rejected with `must NOT have additional properties`. Every example in the official Splunk doc puts it at `containerOptions.visibility`. Same for inputs (`inputs.<id>.containerOptions.visibility`).
2. **Condition expressions use bare tokens + quoted literals.** Write `$selected_host$ = "web-01"`, NOT `"$selected_host$" = "web-01"` (wrapping the token in quotes breaks the SPL parser when the value contains a dash). The `isSet($tok$)` / `not isSet($tok$)` form shown in newer Splunk docs is JSONata-only and is rejected by Splunk Enterprise 10.2.x's SPL-flavoured condition evaluator with `S0201 Syntax error: "isSet"`. Plain `$tok$ != ""` / `$tok$ = ""` works on every version we tested.

Boolean operators are lowercase: `and`, `or`, `not`. Single equals (`=` / `!=`), not `==`.

viz_visibility_a_set.title: A — visible when $selected_host$ is set
viz_visibility_a_set.description: Visible only when `$selected_host$` is non-empty. Uses containerOptions.visibility.showConditions referencing condition_host_set declared in expressions.conditions.

viz_visibility_b_match.title: B — visible only when host == web-01
viz_visibility_b_match.description: Visible only when `$selected_host$` equals 'web-01'. Click that row in §4 to reveal it.

viz_visibility_c_unset.description: Visible only in the initial state (no host selected). Click a row in §4 to hide.

options.markdown: _(C — initial state: no host selected. Click a row in §4 to hide this and reveal A/B.)_
